        elif 'stairs_down_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at map-local position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "You hit a wall" in message_content or "You see no door there" in message_content: # Added more specific wall messages
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()

    if 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info and map_lines:
        re_local_agent_x = perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']
        re_local_agent_y = perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']

        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = re_local_agent_x + dx, re_local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|' or char == '+': # '|' is wall, '+' is closed door by default
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall/closed_door'
                elif char == '-' or char == '{' or char == '}': # '-' are traversable open passages, '{'/'}' also open doors/archways
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'open_passage'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else: 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x_global' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_global' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's global position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_global']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_global']}).")
    elif 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's map-local position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']}).")


    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        if 'stairs_down_x_global' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_global' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at global position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global']}).")
        elif 'stairs_down_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at map-local position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "You hit a wall" in message_content or "You see no door there" in message_content: # Added more specific wall messages
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    global_agent_x, global_agent_y = None, None
    local_agent_x, local_agent_y = None, None
    map_x_offset, map_y_offset = 0, 0 # Offsets to convert global coords to map_lines indices

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
    if agent_coord_match:
        global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(3))
        perceived_info['agent_x'] = global_agent_x
        perceived_info['agent_y'] = global_agent_y

    # Extract the map section and process it
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_lines = map_section_match.group(1).split('\n')

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_y = y_idx
                break
        
        # If global coordinates were available, calculate offsets.
        # Otherwise, the local coordinates are the effective agent coordinates.
        if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
            map_x_offset = global_agent_x - local_agent_x
            map_y_offset = global_agent_y - local_agent_y

        directions = {

            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

            local_nx, local_ny = re_local_agent_x + dx, re_local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:

                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':

                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|' or char == '-':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall'
                elif char == '+':

                elif char == '{' or char == '}': # Treating open doors/archways as distinct from ground
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'door/archway'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':

                elif char == '$':

        elif "wall" in message_content.lower() or re.search(r"You hit a .*? wall", message_content):

        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):

    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    # Agent's canonical global coordinates (from "Yourself a barbarian" line)
    canonical_agent_global_x, canonical_agent_global_y = None, None
    # Agent's local coordinates on the map snippet
    local_agent_map_x, local_agent_map_y = None, None
    
    # Offsets to convert map-local coordinates to global coordinates.
    # These are only valid if canonical global agent coordinates are found.
    map_x_offset, map_y_offset = 0, 0 

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
    if agent_coord_match:
        canonical_agent_global_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        canonical_agent_global_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(3))
        perceived_info['agent_has_global_coords'] = True
        perceived_info['agent_x'] = canonical_agent_global_x
        perceived_info['agent_y'] = canonical_agent_global_y

    # Extract the map section and process it
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_lines = map_section_match.group(1).split('\n')

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_map_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_map_y = y_idx
                perceived_info['agent_has_map_local_coords'] = True
                perceived_info['agent_map_local_x'] = local_agent_map_x
                perceived_info['agent_map_local_y'] = local_agent_map_y
                break
        
        # If canonical global coordinates AND map-local coordinates were found, calculate offsets.
        if canonical_agent_global_x is not None and local_agent_map_x is not None:
            map_x_offset = canonical_agent_global_x - local_agent_map_x
            map_y_offset = canonical_agent_global_y - local_agent_map_y
        # If only map-local coordinates are found, offsets remain 0, meaning map-local coords are effective.

    # Extract the message
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        perceived_info['message'] = message_content

        # Parse nearby items from the message if present
        nearby_items = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - (.*?)(?:\.|$)\s*", message_content)
        if nearby_items:
            # Filter out empty descriptions and strip whitespace
            perceived_info['nearby_items'] = {char: item.strip() for char, item in nearby_items if item.strip()}

        # Check for messages indicating a direction is required
        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    # Check for stairs down (>) presence on the map and extract their coordinates
    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines:
        perceived_info['stairs_down_visible'] = True
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '>' in line:
                local_stairs_x = line.find('>')
                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                # Convert local stairs coordinates to global (if offsets are known) or keep map-local
                perceived_info['stairs_down_x'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                perceived_info['stairs_down_y'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                perceived_info['stairs_are_global_coords'] = (map_x_offset != 0 or map_y_offset != 0)
                break


    # Extract adjacent walkable tiles and obstacles only if agent's *map-local* position is known
    if local_agent_map_x is not None and local_agent_map_y is not None and map_lines:
        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = local_agent_map_x + dx, local_agent_map_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|' or char == '-': # treating single - as wall, consistent with map edges
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall'
                elif char == '+':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'closed door'
                elif char == '{' or char == '}': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'door/archway'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': # Error or unseen entity, should not happen for adjacent
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else: # Catch-all for other map symbols
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if perceived_info.get('agent_has_global_coords'):
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's global position: ({perceived_info['agent_x']}, {perceived_info['agent_y']}).")
    elif perceived_info.get('agent_has_map_local_coords'):
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's map-local position: ({perceived_info['agent_map_local_x']}, {perceived_info['agent_map_local_y']}).")

    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        pos_type = "global position" if perceived_info.get('stairs_are_global_coords') else "map-local position"
        output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at {pos_type}: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y']}).")
        
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "wall" in message_content.lower() or re.search(r"You hit a .*? wall", message_content):
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    global_agent_x, global_agent_y = None, None
    local_agent_x, local_agent_y = None, None
    
    # Offsets to convert global coords to map_lines indices
    # These will be computed only if global agent coords are found.
    # Otherwise, local coordinates are considered the primary reference.
    map_x_offset = 0
    map_y_offset = 0

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian(?:an)?\s*\((?:x=(\d+), )?y=(\d+)\)", observation_text) # Corrected regex for optional x=
    if agent_coord_match:
        # Check if x is present. Sometimes only y is given (unlikely but robust).
        if agent_coord_match.group(1): # if x coordinate is present
            global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(1))
            global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        else: # only y present if it matches, handle as error or special case for now default to current
            # For simplicity, assume both x and y are typically present if this regex is used.
            # If only Y is matched, it means the pattern was `(y=XXX)`. This is unlikely for agent's position.
            # Original: r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)" which requires both. Sticking to original for now.
             agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
             if agent_coord_match: # Re-match with original
                 global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
                 global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(3))


    # Extract the map section and process it
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_lines = map_section_match.group(1).split('\n')

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_y = y_idx
                break
        
        # Determine the agent's effective coordinates for the current turn and reference flag
        if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
            perceived_info['agent_x'] = global_agent_x
            perceived_info['agent_y'] = global_agent_y
            perceived_info['coords_type'] = 'global'
            map_x_offset = global_agent_x - local_agent_x
            map_y_offset = global_agent_y - local_agent_y
        elif local_agent_x is not None:
            perceived_info['agent_x'] = local_agent_x
            perceived_info['agent_y'] = local_agent_y
            perceived_info['coords_type'] = 'map-local'
            # If only local coords are available, offsets are 0 as local is the reference
            map_x_offset = 0
            map_y_offset = 0

    # Extract the message
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        perceived_info['message'] = message_content

        # Parse nearby items from the message if present
        nearby_items = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - (.*?)(?:\.|$)\s*", message_content)
        if nearby_items:
            # Filter out empty descriptions and strip whitespace
            perceived_info['nearby_items'] = {char: item.strip() for char, item in nearby_items if item.strip()}

        # Check for messages indicating a direction is required
        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    # Check for stairs down (>) presence on the map and extract their coordinates
    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines and 'agent_x' in perceived_info: # Only process stairs if agent coords were found and map exists
        perceived_info['stairs_down_visible'] = True
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '>' in line:
                local_stairs_x = line.find('>')
                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                # Convert local stairs coordinates to global (if applicable, else local)
                perceived_info['stairs_down_x'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                perceived_info['stairs_down_y'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                perceived_info['stairs_down_coords_type'] = perceived_info.get('coords_type', 'map-local') # Inherit type or default
                break


    # Extract adjacent walkable tiles and obstacles only if agent's position is known
    if 'agent_x' in perceived_info and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None: # Use local_agent_x/y for *relative* map movements
        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = local_agent_x + dx, local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '+':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'closed door'
                elif char == '{' or char == '}': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'door/archway'
                elif char == '|' or char == '-': # '---' is a pattern, single char is typically wall
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent (error or unseen entity)' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else:
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x' in perceived_info and 'agent_y' in perceived_info:
        coords_type = perceived_info.get('coords_type', 'unknown')
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's {coords_type} position: ({perceived_info['agent_x']}, {perceived_info['agent_y']}).")
    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        if 'stairs_down_x' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y' in perceived_info:
            coords_type = perceived_info.get('stairs_down_coords_type', 'unknown')
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at {coords_type} position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "wall" in message_content.lower() or re.search(r"You hit a .*? wall", message_content) or re.search(r"You can't move through the solid rock", message_content):
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    global_agent_x, global_agent_y = None, None
    local_agent_x, local_agent_y = None, None
    
    map_x_offset = 0
    map_y_offset = 0

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    # Matches "Yourself a barbarian (x=26, y=10)"
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
    if agent_coord_match:
        global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(3))

    # Extract the map section and process it
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_lines = map_section_match.group(1).split('\n')

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_y = y_idx
                break
        
        # Determine the agent's effective coordinates and reference flag
        if global_agent_x is not None and global_agent_y is not None:
            perceived_info['agent_x'] = global_agent_x
            perceived_info['agent_y'] = global_agent_y
            perceived_info['coords_type'] = 'global'
            if local_agent_x is not None: # If local map is also found, calculate offsets
                map_x_offset = global_agent_x - local_agent_x
                map_y_offset = global_agent_y - local_agent_y
        elif local_agent_x is not None and local_agent_y is not None: # Only local map coords available for agent
            perceived_info['agent_x'] = local_agent_x
            perceived_info['agent_y'] = local_agent_y
            perceived_info['coords_type'] = 'map-local'
            # map_x_offset and map_y_offset remain 0, meaning local coordinates are the effective ones
            # for any other map features like stairs.

    # Extract the message
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        perceived_info['message'] = message_content

        nearby_items = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - (.*?)(?:\.|$)\s*", message_content)
        if nearby_items:
            perceived_info['nearby_items'] = {char: item.strip() for char, item in nearby_items if item.strip()}

        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    # Check for stairs down (>) presence on the map and extract their coordinates
    # Only try to calculate stairs coordinates if agent's effective coords are established and map is present
    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines and 'agent_x' in perceived_info: 
        perceived_info['stairs_down_visible'] = True
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '>' in line:
                local_stairs_x = line.find('>')
                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                
                # Apply computed offsets. If no global context, offsets are 0, so local coords are used.
                perceived_info['stairs_down_x'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                perceived_info['stairs_down_y'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                
                # Inherit coordinate type from agent's position for consistency, or default to map-local
                perceived_info['stairs_down_coords_type'] = perceived_info.get('coords_type', 'map-local') 
                break


    # Extract adjacent walkable tiles and obstacles only if agent's *local* position is known from map parsing
    if local_agent_x is not None and local_agent_y is not None and map_lines:
        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = local_agent_x + dx, local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '+':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'closed door'
                elif char == '{' or char == '}': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'door/archway'
                elif char == '|' or char == '-': # '---' is a pattern, single char is typically wall
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent (error or unseen entity)' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void' # Could be unexplored or outside current map view extent
                else:
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x' in perceived_info and 'agent_y' in perceived_info:
        coords_type = perceived_info.get('coords_type', 'unknown')
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's {coords_type} position: ({perceived_info['agent_x']}, {perceived_info['agent_y']}).")
    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        if 'stairs_down_x' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y' in perceived_info:
            coords_type = perceived_info.get('stairs_down_coords_type', 'unknown')
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at {coords_type} position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "wall" in message_content.lower() or re.search(r"You hit a .*? wall", message_content) or re.search(r"(?:You can't move through the solid rock|Blocked by the .*? wall)", message_content):
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()
```
Added `Blocked by the .*? wall` for wall detection, which is another common message. The `if 'agent_x' in perceived_info` for stairs was changed to `if local_agent_x is not None` because `perceived_info['agent_x']` might *not* be set if agent is not on map, but still, if stairs and agent are both on map, then local_agent_x should be present. The condition `local_agent_x is not None` is more direct about map presence. And the map exists already due to `map_lines`.

Actually, better to keep `if 'agent_x' in perceived_info` for stairs for consistency using the already established agent coordinates (`perceived_info['agent_x']` will be set if either global or local map agent coords were successfully parsed). This ensures stair coordinates are relative to the agent's known position.

Reverted the change to `if 'agent_x' in perceived_info` for stairs conditional check.
The `adjacent_cells` check was `if 'agent_x' in perceived_info and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None:` - this is fine, it means if we have *any* agent position knowledge and *also* found the agent locally on the map (which is needed for relative adjacent cell calculations).

Final logic seems sound. The main fix is carefully handling `coords_type` and `map_x_offset/y_offset` so that coordinates correctly reflect global if available, or map-local if not. And then using the `coords_type` correctly in the output string. This would be a more accurate perception given the RAW observation.

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    global_agent_x, global_agent_y = None, None
    local_agent_x, local_agent_y = None, None
    map_x_offset, map_y_offset = 0, 0 # Offsets to convert global coords to map_lines indices

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    # The 'cursor' line often has an (x,y) but it's not always the agent's (e.g., step 4)
    # The "Yourself a barbarian (x=26, y=10)" line is the most reliable for global agent coords.
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a \w+\s*\((?:x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
    if agent_coord_match:
        global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(1))
        global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        perceived_info['agent_x'] = global_agent_x
        perceived_info['agent_y'] = global_agent_y
        perceived_info['agent_coord_type'] = 'global'

    # Extract the map section and process it
    # Use a non-greedy match for the map section to prevent it from consuming parts of other sections
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_lines = map_section_match.group(1).split('\n')
        # Filter out empty lines from the map_lines after splitting
        map_lines = [line for line in map_lines if line.strip()]

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_y = y_idx
                break
        
        # If global coordinates were available, calculate offsets.
        # Otherwise, the local coordinates are the effective agent coordinates.
        if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
            map_x_offset = global_agent_x - local_agent_x
            map_y_offset = global_agent_y - local_agent_y
        elif local_agent_x is not None: # No canonical global coords, use local as effective
            # If global wasn't found, use local as pseudo-global for relative calculations from now on
            perceived_info['agent_x'] = local_agent_x
            perceived_info['agent_y'] = local_agent_y
            perceived_info['agent_coord_type'] = 'map-local'
            global_agent_x = local_agent_x # Set for consistent use later (e.g. adjacent cells)
            global_agent_y = local_agent_y # Set for consistent use later

    # Extract the message
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        perceived_info['message'] = message_content

        # Parse nearby items from the message if present
        nearby_items = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - (.*?)(?:\.|$)\s*", message_content)
        if nearby_items:
            # Filter out empty descriptions and strip whitespace
            perceived_info['nearby_items'] = {char: item.strip() for char, item in nearby_items if item.strip()}

        # Check for messages indicating a direction is required
        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    # Check for stairs down (>) presence on the map and extract their coordinates
    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None: # Ensure agent is found locally to proceed
        perceived_info['stairs_down_visible'] = True
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '>' in line:
                local_stairs_x = line.find('>')
                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                # Convert local stairs coordinates to global
                if perceived_info.get('agent_coord_type') == 'global':
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_x'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_y'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                    perceived_info['stairs_coord_type'] = 'global'
                else: # Use map-local if no global agent coords were available
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_x'] = local_stairs_x
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_y'] = local_stairs_y
                    perceived_info['stairs_coord_type'] = 'map-local'
                break


    # Extract adjacent walkable tiles and obstacles only if agent's position is known
    if 'agent_x' in perceived_info and 'agent_y' in perceived_info and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None:
        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = local_agent_x + dx, local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|': # Vertical wall (impassable)
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall'
                elif char == '-': # Horizontal passage (traversable based on world knowledge)
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'open_passage'
                elif char == '+':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'closed door'
                elif char == '{' or char == '}': # Treating open doors/archways as distinct from ground
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'door/archway'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': # This should technically not happen for an adjacent cell if offsets are correct
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent (error or unseen entity)' # Keep for debug
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else:
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x' in perceived_info and 'agent_y' in perceived_info:
        coord_type = perceived_info.get('agent_coord_type', 'unknown')
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's {coord_type} position: ({perceived_info['agent_x']}, {perceived_info['agent_y']}).")
    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        if 'stairs_down_x' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y' in perceived_info:
            coord_type = perceived_info.get('stairs_coord_type', 'unknown')
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at {coord_type} position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "wall" in message_content.lower() or re.search(r"You hit a .*? wall", message_content):
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()
```
The change made is to classify `'-'` as `open_passage` instead of `wall` in the `adjacent_cells` logic, to adhere strictly to the World Knowledge: "Horizontal `---` symbols on the map represent traversable open passages." This addresses the previous point of ambiguity. The output for `|` remains `wall`.

The `map_lines = [line for line in map_lines if line.strip()]` was also added to ensure empty lines sometimes present at the start or end of the map section after splitting are removed, which could affect local coordinate calculation for `@` and `>`. This is an improvement for robustness.
Added a check `and local_agent_x is not None` to the stairs down section to ensure local agent coords are found before attempting to calculate stairs coords based on map offsets. This prevents errors if `@` is not on the map (e.g., if the agent died or the map section is malformed).

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    global_agent_x, global_agent_y = None, None
    local_agent_x, local_agent_y = None, None
    map_x_offset, map_y_offset = 0, 0 # Offsets to convert global coords to map_lines indices

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
    if agent_coord_match:
        global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(3))
        perceived_info['agent_x_global'] = global_agent_x
        perceived_info['agent_y_global'] = global_agent_y

    # Extract the map section and process it
    # Use a non-greedy match for map content and ensure it ends before "Agent the" or end of string
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_content = map_section_match.group(1).strip()
        map_lines = map_content.split('\n')

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_y = y_idx
                perceived_info['agent_x_map_local'] = local_agent_x
                perceived_info['agent_y_map_local'] = local_agent_y
                break
        
        # If global coordinates were available AND local coordinates were found, calculate offsets for global coordination
        if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
            map_x_offset = global_agent_x - local_agent_x
            map_y_offset = global_agent_y - local_agent_y
        
    # Extract the message
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        perceived_info['message'] = message_content

        # Parse nearby items from the message if present
        nearby_items = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - (.*?)(?:\.|$)\s*", message_content)
        if nearby_items:
            # Filter out empty descriptions and strip whitespace
            perceived_info['nearby_items'] = {char: item.strip() for char, item in nearby_items if item.strip()}

        # Check for messages indicating a direction is required
        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    # Check for stairs down (>) presence on the map and extract their coordinates
    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None: # Ensure agent is found for local map context
        perceived_info['stairs_down_visible'] = True
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '>' in line:
                local_stairs_x = line.find('>')
                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                
                perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local'] = local_stairs_x
                perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local'] = local_stairs_y

                # Convert local stairs coordinates to global if map_x_offset/map_y_offset were calculated
                if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                break


    # Extract adjacent walkable tiles and obstacles only if agent's local position on map is known
    if 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info and map_lines:
        re_local_agent_x = perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']
        re_local_agent_y = perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']

        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = re_local_agent_x + dx, re_local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|' or char == '+': # '|' is wall, '+' is closed door by default
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall/closed_door'
                elif char == '-' or char == '{' or char == '}': # '-' are traversable open passages, '{'/'}' also open doors/archways
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'open_passage'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else: 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x_global' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_global' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's global position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_global']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_global']}).")
    elif 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's map-local position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']}).")


    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        if 'stairs_down_x_global' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_global' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at global position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global']}).")
        elif 'stairs_down_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at map-local position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "You hit a wall" in message_content or "You see no door there" in message_content: # Added more specific wall messages
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()
```

        # Check for messages indicating a direction is required
        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None: # Ensure agent is found for local map context

        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):

                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                
                perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local'] = local_stairs_x
                perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local'] = local_stairs_y

                # Convert local stairs coordinates to global if map_x_offset/map_y_offset were calculated
                if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                break

        re_local_agent_y = perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']

        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {

            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = re_local_agent_x + dx, re_local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|' or char == '+': # '|' is wall, '+' is closed door by default
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall/closed_door'
                elif char == '-' or char == '{' or char == '}': # '-' are traversable open passages, '{'/'}' also open doors/archways
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'open_passage'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else: 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x_global' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_global' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's global position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_global']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_global']}).")
    elif 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's map-local position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']}).")


    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):

            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at global position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global']}).")
        elif 'stairs_down_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at map-local position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "You hit a wall" in message_content or "You see no door there" in message_content: # Added more specific wall messages
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    global_agent_x, global_agent_y = None, None
    local_agent_x, local_agent_y = None, None
    map_x_offset, map_y_offset = 0, 0 # Offsets to convert global coords to map_lines indices

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
    if agent_coord_match:
        global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(3))
        perceived_info['agent_x_global'] = global_agent_x
        perceived_info['agent_y_global'] = global_agent_y

    # Extract the map section and process it
    # Use a non-greedy match for map content and ensure it ends before "Agent the" or end of string
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_content = map_section_match.group(1).strip()
        map_lines = map_content.split('\n')

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_y = y_idx
                perceived_info['agent_x_map_local'] = local_agent_x
                perceived_info['agent_y_map_local'] = local_agent_y
                break
        
        # If global coordinates were available AND local coordinates were found, calculate offsets for global coordination
        if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
            map_x_offset = global_agent_x - local_agent_x
            map_y_offset = global_agent_y - local_agent_y
        
    # Extract the message
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        perceived_info['message'] = message_content

        # Parse nearby items from the message if present
        nearby_items = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - (.*?)(?:\.|$)\s*", message_content)
        if nearby_items:
            # Filter out empty descriptions and strip whitespace
            perceived_info['nearby_items'] = {char: item.strip() for char, item in nearby_items if item.strip()}

        # Check for messages indicating a direction is required
        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    # Check for stairs down (>) presence on the map and extract their coordinates
    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None: # Ensure agent is found for local map context
        perceived_info['stairs_down_visible'] = True
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '>' in line:
                local_stairs_x = line.find('>')
                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                
                perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local'] = local_stairs_x
                perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local'] = local_stairs_y

                # Convert local stairs coordinates to global if map_x_offset/map_y_offset were calculated
                if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                break


    # Extract adjacent walkable tiles and obstacles only if agent's local position on map is known
    if 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info and map_lines:
        re_local_agent_x = perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']
        re_local_agent_y = perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']

        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = re_local_agent_x + dx, re_local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|' or char == '+': # '|' is wall, '+' is closed door by default
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall/closed_door'
                elif char == '-' or char == '{' or char == '}': # '-' are traversable open passages, '{'/'}' also open doors/archways
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'open_passage'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else: 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x_global' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_global' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's global position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_global']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_global']}).")
    elif 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's map-local position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']}).")


    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        if 'stairs_down_x_global' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_global' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at global position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global']}).")
        elif 'stairs_down_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at map-local position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "You hit a wall" in message_content or "You see no door there" in message_content: # Added more specific wall messages
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()
```
</updated_perception>

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    perceived_info = {}

    global_agent_x, global_agent_y = None, None
    local_agent_x, local_agent_y = None, None
    map_x_offset, map_y_offset = 0, 0 # Offsets to convert global coords to map_lines indices

    # 1. Extract agent's canonical global coordinates (x, y) if available
    agent_coord_match = re.search(r"Yourself a barbarian\s*\((x=(\d+), y=(\d+))\)", observation_text)
    if agent_coord_match:
        global_agent_x = int(agent_coord_match.group(2))
        global_agent_y = int(agent_coord_match.group(3))
        perceived_info['agent_x_global'] = global_agent_x
        perceived_info['agent_y_global'] = global_agent_y

    # Extract the map section and process it
    # Use a non-greedy match for map content and ensure it ends before "Agent the" or end of string
    map_section_match = re.search(r"map:\n(.*?)(?=\nAgent the|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    map_lines = []
    if map_section_match:
        map_content = map_section_match.group(1).strip()
        map_lines = map_content.split('\n')

        # 2. Find agent's local coordinates (within the map_lines list)
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '@' in line:
                local_agent_x = line.find('@')
                local_agent_y = y_idx
                perceived_info['agent_x_map_local'] = local_agent_x
                perceived_info['agent_y_map_local'] = local_agent_y
                break
        
        # If global coordinates were available AND local coordinates were found, calculate offsets for global coordination
        if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
            map_x_offset = global_agent_x - local_agent_x
            map_y_offset = global_agent_y - local_agent_y
        
    # Extract the message
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
        perceived_info['message'] = message_content

        # Parse nearby items from the message if present
        nearby_items = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - (.*?)(?:\.|$)\s*", message_content)
        if nearby_items:
            # Filter out empty descriptions and strip whitespace
            perceived_info['nearby_items'] = {char: item.strip() for char, item in nearby_items if item.strip()}

        # Check for messages indicating a direction is required
        if "In what direction?" in message_content:
            perceived_info['action_requires_direction'] = True

    # Check for stairs down (>) presence on the map and extract their coordinates
    if '>' in observation_text and map_lines and local_agent_x is not None: # Ensure agent is found for local map context
        perceived_info['stairs_down_visible'] = True
        for y_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
            if '>' in line:
                local_stairs_x = line.find('>')
                local_stairs_y = y_idx
                
                perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local'] = local_stairs_x
                perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local'] = local_stairs_y

                # Convert local stairs coordinates to global if map_x_offset/map_y_offset were calculated
                if global_agent_x is not None and local_agent_x is not None:
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global'] = local_stairs_x + map_x_offset
                    perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global'] = local_stairs_y + map_y_offset
                break


    # Extract adjacent walkable tiles and obstacles only if agent's local position on map is known
    if 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info and map_lines:
        re_local_agent_x = perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']
        re_local_agent_y = perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']

        adjacent_cells = {}
        directions = {
            'north': (0, -1), 'northeast': (1, -1), 'east': (1, 0), 'southeast': (1, 1),
            'south': (0, 1), 'southwest': (-1, 1), 'west': (-1, 0), 'northwest': (-1, -1)
        }

        h = len(map_lines)
        w = len(map_lines[0]) if h > 0 else 0

        for direction, (dx, dy) in directions.items():
            local_nx, local_ny = re_local_agent_x + dx, re_local_agent_y + dy

            if 0 <= local_ny < h and 0 <= local_nx < w:
                char = map_lines[local_ny][local_nx]
                # Interpret characters
                if char == '.':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'ground'
                elif char == '#':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'corridor'
                elif char == '|' or char == '+': # '|' is wall, '+' is closed door by default
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'wall/closed_door'
                elif char == '-' or char == '{' or char == '}': # '-' are traversable open passages, '{'/'}' also open doors/archways
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'open_passage'
                elif char == '^':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'trap'
                elif char == '~':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'water/moat'
                elif char == '%':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'lava'
                elif char == '$':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'gold'
                elif char == '@': 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'agent' 
                elif char == '>':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs down'
                elif char == '<':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'stairs up'
                elif char == ' ':
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = 'empty/void'
                else: 
                    adjacent_cells[direction] = f'item/feature ({char})'
        perceived_info['adjacent_cells'] = adjacent_cells

    # Convert to a string representation for the agent
    output_str_parts = []
    if 'agent_x_global' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_global' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's global position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_global']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_global']}).")
    elif 'agent_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'agent_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
        output_str_parts.append(f"Agent's map-local position: ({perceived_info['agent_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['agent_y_map_local']}).")


    if perceived_info.get('stairs_down_visible'):
        if 'stairs_down_x_global' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_global' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at global position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_global']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_global']}).")
        elif 'stairs_down_x_map_local' in perceived_info and 'stairs_down_y_map_local' in perceived_info:
            output_str_parts.append(f"Stairs down (>) are visible at map-local position: ({perceived_info['stairs_down_x_map_local']}, {perceived_info['stairs_down_y_map_local']}).")
        else:
            output_str_parts.append("Stairs down (>) are visible somewhere on the map (coordinates unknown).")
    
    if 'message' in perceived_info:
        message_content = perceived_info['message']
        output_str_parts.append(f"Last message: \"{message_content}\"")
        if "lava" in message_content.lower() or "burned by molten lava" in message_content.lower():
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Lava.")
        elif "You hit a wall" in message_content or "You see no door there" in message_content: # Added more specific wall messages
            output_str_parts.append("Obstacle detected: Wall.")
        
        # Add a flag if an action requires direction
        if perceived_info.get('action_requires_direction'):
            output_str_parts.append("Action requires direction input.")
            
    if 'nearby_items' in perceived_info:
        items_str = ", ".join([f"{char}: {item}" for char, item in perceived_info['nearby_items'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Nearby items: {items_str}.")

    if 'adjacent_cells' in perceived_info:
        adjacent_str = ", ".join([f"{d}: {c}" for d, c in perceived_info['adjacent_cells'].items()])
        output_str_parts.append(f"Adjacent cells: {adjacent_str}.")

    return "\n".join(output_str_parts).strip()
